extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each entity: I counted only the Against rows, giving criminal proceedings of 1 against the Company, 1 against Subsidiaries, 2 against Directors, 2 against Promoters, 2 against Key Management Personnel and 0 against Senior Management; statutory/regulatory proceedings of 1, 2, 1, 1, 1 and 0 respectively; and tax proceedings of 1 against the Company plus 22 against Subsidiaries, excluding all By rows and promoter/director tax matters from the tax count. For the aggregate quantified amount against the Company and Subsidiaries, I summed the disclosed Against amounts of ₹44.03 million and ₹1,300.91 million, totaling ₹1,344.94 million, or ₹134.494 crore; the table states these amounts are only to the extent ascertainable and quantifiable. The contingent-liabilities figure is derived from the disclosed September 30, 2025 Ind AS 37 amounts, using the stated tax total of ₹104.61 million plus GST demands of ₹1,119.01 million and ₹121.38 million, totaling ₹1,345.00 million or ₹134.50 crore, excluding the separately disclosed corporate guarantees. The concentration disclosures are not top-five or top-ten customer figures; the company-wide percentages disclosed relate to states, payors, specialties and related parties, so customer concentration is not disclosed. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million; conversion to crore is by dividing by 10 (₹10 million = ₹1 crore).
